DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF ERIKA MCCARTNEY'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. PLAINTIFF ERIKA MCCARTNEY'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, SERVED ON DEFENDANT PACIFIC WEST INGREDIENTS, LLC ("PACIFIC"), IS GRANTED. VERIFIED, CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES ARE DUE TO BE RECEIVED IN PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S OFFICE WITHIN TWO WEEKS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,280 ARE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF AGAINST PACIFIC. SANCTIONS ARE PAYABLE WITHIN TWO WEEKS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST SEND AN ELECTRONIC COPY OF THE PROPOSED ORDER DIRECTLY TO JUDGE PRO TEM AT PCATALANOTTI@FMGLAW.COM, CC'ING ALL OTHER COUNSEL. JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER CATALANOTTI, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (D302/JPT/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions; Points And Authorities; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Feb-15-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF ERIKA MCCARTNEY's Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions, as follows:

Plaintiff Erika McCartney's unopposed Motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatories and for Monetary Sanctions is granted. Defendant Pacific West Ingredients, LCC must provide verified, code-compliant responses, without any objections except as to attorney-client privilege, attorney work product, or privacy. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,140 are awarded in favor of plaintiff against defendant. The responses shall be served and the sanctions shall be paid by March 6, 2018.

The moving party is to submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling. Judge Pro Tem: Melinda Derish; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Inspection Demands And For Monetary Sanctions; Points And Authorities; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Feb-15-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF ERIKA MCCARTNEY's Motion To Compel Responses To Inspection Demands And For Monetary Sanctions, as follows:

Plaintiff Erika McCartney's unopposed Motion to Compel Responses to Inspection Demands and for Monetary Sanctions is granted. Defendant Pacific West Ingredients, LCC must produce all responsive non-privileged documents in their possession, custody or control. To the extent that defendant withholds any responsive documents, defendant is ordered to identify each document being withheld, the basis for asserting the claim of privilege, and provide a privilege log. As to each specific request for production of documents defendant shall provide a code compliant verification affirming that it has made a diligent search to produce all responsive documents. Sanctions in the amount of $1,380 are awarded in favor of plaintiff against defendant. The document production and privilege log shall be served and the sanctions shall be paid by March 6, 2018.

The moving party is to submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling. Judge Pro Tem: Melinda Derish; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions; Points And Authorities; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Jan-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ERIKA MCCARTNEY's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions.

Plaintiff Erika McCartney's unopposed Motion to Compel Responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One, served on Defendant Pacific West Ingredients, LLC ("Pacific"), is GRANTED. Verified, Code-Compliant responses are due to be received in plaintiff's counsel's office within two weeks of notice of entry of this order. Sanctions in the amount of $2,280 are awarded in favor of plaintiff against Pacific. Sanctions are payable within two weeks of notice of entry of this order.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must send an electronic copy of the proposed order directly to Judge Pro Tem at pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com, cc'ing all other Counsel.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/JPT/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions; Points And Authorities; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Nov-17-2017 Continued To Dec-05-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ERIKA MCCARTNEY's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions is Continued to December 5, 2017 at 9 a.m. to give the moving party a chance to comply with SF Local Rule 2.7B. All parties shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of their filings to Dept. 301 with a cover letter that reflects the new hearing date. The Court also notes that the Declaration of Robert Hancock referenced in the moving papers is not on the Register of Actions and should be filed immediately.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/JPT/HK)
